assumptions: The intragroup dividend has already created a recognised monetary receivable/payable exposure.
The receivable/payable is exposed to foreign currency risk because the entities involved have different functional currencies.
The resulting foreign exchange gains or losses are not fully eliminated on consolidation, so the intragroup monetary item can qualify as a hedged item in consolidated financial statements.

approaches.label: Fair value hedge of foreign currency risk
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: This is relevant because IFRS 9 treats a recognised asset or liability as eligible for a fair value hedge, and paragraph 6.3.6 specifically permits foreign currency risk on an intragroup monetary item in consolidated financial statements when it affects consolidated profit or loss.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge of foreign currency risk
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: This is relevant because IFRS 9 identifies cash flow hedge accounting as a separate hedge model for variability in cash flows associated with a recognised asset or liability attributable to a particular risk.

approaches.label: Net investment hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: This is relevant because, if the group documents the exposure as foreign currency risk on a net investment in a foreign operation rather than on the receivable itself, IFRS 9 and IFRIC 16 provide a distinct hedge accounting model with OCI presentation.
